# Bobby G. Rice

> Country singer-songwriter with 30 charted Billboard singles, two CMA Award nominations (1973), and the #1 country hit "You Lay So Easy On My Mind." Boscobel, Wisconsin native who has been a fixture in country music for over six decades.

## Quick facts
- Full name: Bobby G. Rice
- Genre: Country music
- Career: 1960s — present (60+ years)
- Birthplace: Boscobel, Wisconsin, USA
- Career highlights: 30 Billboard-charted singles; biggest hit "You Lay So Easy On My Mind" reached #1 on the Billboard Country chart; two CMA Award nominations in 1973 (Single of the Year + Best Male Vocalist); recorded on GRT Records
- Still actively performing: yes
- Official website: https://www.bobbygrice.com

## Notable songs
- "You Lay So Easy On My Mind" (1973) — #1 Billboard Country
- "Mainstream of Memories"
- "You Give Me You"
- "You Are What Made Me Love You"

## Frequently asked questions

### What is Bobby G. Rice's biggest hit?
"You Lay So Easy On My Mind" — released in 1973, it reached #1 on the Billboard Country chart and earned him CMA Award nominations.

### When was Bobby G. Rice nominated for the CMA Awards?
1973 — nominated for Single of the Year ("You Lay So Easy On My Mind") and Best Male Vocalist of the Year.

### Where is Bobby G. Rice from?
Boscobel, Wisconsin.

### Is Bobby G. Rice still performing?
Yes. He continues to tour and record.

## Links
- Official site: https://www.bobbygrice.com
- Genre: Country / Nashville
